response:
To compute the **sample standard deviation** of the data set:

$$
\{1, 12, -17, -3, -11, -5\}
$$

---

### Step 1: Find the mean (average) of the data set

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{1 + 12 + (-17) + (-3) + (-11) + (-5)}{6}
$$

$$
= \frac{1 + 12 - 17 - 3 - 11 - 5}{6}
= \frac{-19}{6}
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the squared differences from the mean

$$
(1 - (-\frac{19}{6}))^2 = \left(\frac{25}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{625}{36}
$$

$$
(12 - (-\frac{19}{6}))^2 = \left(\frac{91}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{8281}{36}
$$

$$
(-17 - (-\frac{19}{6}))^2 = \left(-\frac{95}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{9025}{36}
$$

$$
(-3 - (-\frac{19}{6}))^2 = \left(\frac{13}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{169}{36}
$$

$$
(-11 - (-\frac{19}{6}))^2 = \left(-\frac{43}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{1849}{36}
$$

$$
(-5 - (-\frac{19}{6}))^2 = \left(\frac{9}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{81}{36}
$$

---

### Step 3: Sum the squared differences

$$
\text{Sum} = \frac{625 + 8281 + 9025 + 169 + 1849 + 81}{36}
$$

$$
= \frac{28200}{36}
$$

---
